title: Blessed Assurance
author: Fanny Crosby

scriptureRefs.note: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith
The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God

story: Fanny Crosby, who was blind from infancy and wrote over 8,000 hymns, composed this text in 1873 when her friend Phoebe Palmer Knapp played a new melody on the piano and asked, 'What does this tune say?' Crosby immediately replied, 'Blessed assurance, Jesus is mine!' and wrote the lyrics on the spot. The hymn captures the joyful certainty of salvation that characterized Crosby's remarkable faith despite her lifelong blindness.

lines: Blessed assurance, Jesus is mine!
O what a foretaste of glory divine!
Heir of salvation, purchase of God,
Born of His Spirit, washed in His blood.
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.
Perfect submission, perfect delight,
Visions of rapture now burst on my sight;
Angels descending bring from above
Echoes of mercy, whispers of love.
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.
Perfect submission, all is at rest;
I in my Saviour am happy and blest,
Watching and waiting, looking above,
Filled with His goodness, lost in His love.
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.
